page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor architecture with 32 physical cores and 2 threads per core, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated matrix operations, and cryptographic accelerations like AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the n4-highmem-64 demonstrates strong memory performance, with uncached read bandwidth reaching 276.11 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 121.94 GB/sec. Compute performance is highlighted by a Passmark CPU score of 62,986.78 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17,904.0. The instance excels in static web serving throughput, achieving a top-tier result of 88.31 GB/sec, and image processing with a score of 17,489.0. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 29,375.76 and Redis SET operations performing at 70.51 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference capabilities process prompts at 224.22 tokens/sec on medium 7B models.

The qualitative cost profile of the n4-highmem-64 reflects its high resource density, offering a substantial 8 GB of RAM per vCPU. This memory-to-core ratio makes it highly efficient for memory-bound applications without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe SSD storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which represents a potential I/O latency tradeoff for disk-heavy tasks. The instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale database hosting, in-memory caching with Redis, software compilation, static web serving at scale, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 64 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, driven by an Intel x86_64 processor with 32 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache. The architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark results show strong memory bandwidth, with uncached read speeds of 276.11 GB/sec, and top-tier performance in static web serving at 88.31 GB/sec and image processing. Database operations are highly efficient, supported by Redis SET performance of 70.51 million operations per second. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance represents a high-density memory configuration. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and Intel x86_64 architecture, delivering strong database, web serving, and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-64 is an Intel-powered memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring sixty-four vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for demanding database and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM providing a high-density 1:8 memory-to-core ratio.
Intel x86_64 architecture with AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).
Strong memory read bandwidth of 276.11 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 121.94 GB/sec.
Top-tier static web serving throughput of 88.31 GB/sec and high Redis performance.
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and CPU-based LLM inference.
No local storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.
